text: K values are restricted to the following set: {4, 8, 16, 24, 32, 64, 96, 128}. Do NOT use any other K value. This applies to all experiments regardless of architecture or family.
EXPANSION_FACTOR is now fixed at 16 for all experiments. Do NOT use EF=8 or EF=12. Set EXPANSION_FACTOR=16 in every env_overrides.

EXPLORATION PRIORITY SHIFT: Stop inventing new architecture variants. The architecture search has plateaued after 117 rounds. Instead, shift focus to these under-explored dimensions:

1. Loss function (loss_fn): explore alternatives to the default FVU loss
2. Optimizer: explore alternatives to signum
3. Data preprocessing: explore activation transformations (e.g. USE_HADAMARD)

You may use ANY existing registered architecture — not just current frontier holders. An architecture that previously underperformed with FVU+signum may excel under a different loss or optimizer. Make reasoned choices about which architecture to pair with which training recipe, and explain your reasoning.

When probing an alternative optimizer, do NOT conclude the optimizer is ineffective from a single swap with the old learning rate held fixed. Optimizer changes can require retuning learning rate. If an optimizer underperforms on the first try, consider whether LR mismatch may be the cause and allow follow-up LR adjustment experiments before discarding that optimizer. In these cases, inspect the training-curve shape as well, not just the final FVU/F value: distinguish instability, overshooting, late improvement, and smooth but weak convergence before deciding whether the issue is optimizer choice or learning-rate mismatch. Do not use a fixed canned LR list; choose the adjustment strategy autonomously based on the observed behavior and the current backbone.
